# debian_systemd_service (Resource)

`debian_systemd_service` manages a systemd `.service` unit on the remote host. Supports both package-installed services (state management only) and custom services (unit file + state management).



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `active` (Boolean) Whether the service is running (`systemctl start` / `stop`).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the service is enabled (`systemctl enable` / `disable`).
- `name` (String) Service unit name (without `.service` extension).
- `ssh` (Attributes) SSH connection configuration. Authentication can be specified here or inherited from the provider's `private_key` / `private_keys`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ssh))

### Optional

- `install` (Attributes) `[Install]` section. Optional — omit for package-installed services. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--install))
- `service` (Attributes) `[Service]` section. Optional — omit for package-installed services. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service))
- `unit` (Attributes) `[Unit]` section. Optional — omit for package-installed services. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--unit))

<a id="nestedatt--ssh"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ssh`

Required:

- `hostname` (String) The remote host to connect to.

Optional:

- `host_key` (String) Public key of the remote host in authorized_keys format. If set, the server's identity is verified against this key. If not set, all host keys are accepted (insecure).
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password to authenticate the user on the remote host. Conflicts with `private_key` and `public_key`.
- `port` (Number) Port that the remote host uses for ssh.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) Private key to authenticate with the remote host. Conflicts with `password` and `public_key`. When omitted, the provider's `private_key` is used.
- `public_key` (String) Public key whose corresponding private key is looked up in the provider's `private_keys` map. Conflicts with `private_key` and `password`.
- `user` (String) User on the remote host, the default is `root`.


<a id="nestedatt--install"></a>
### Nested Schema for `install`

Optional:

- `alias` (List of String) `Alias=` — alternative names for the unit.
- `extra` (Map of String) Additional `[Install]` directives. Keys must use exact systemd directive names.
- `required_by` (List of String) `RequiredBy=` — targets that require this service when enabled.
- `wanted_by` (List of String) `WantedBy=` — targets that pull in this service when enabled.


<a id="nestedatt--service"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service`

Required:

- `exec_start` (String) `ExecStart=` — command to run.

Optional:

- `environment` (Map of String) `Environment=` — env vars. Each entry becomes `Environment="K=V"`.
- `environment_file` (String) `EnvironmentFile=` — path to an env file.
- `exec_reload` (String) `ExecReload=` — command to reload configuration.
- `exec_start_post` (List of String) `ExecStartPost=` — commands to run after ExecStart.
- `exec_start_pre` (List of String) `ExecStartPre=` — commands to run before ExecStart.
- `exec_stop` (String) `ExecStop=` — command to stop the service.
- `exec_stop_post` (List of String) `ExecStopPost=` — commands to run after the service stops.
- `extra` (Map of String) Additional `[Service]` directives. Keys must use exact systemd directive names.
- `group` (String) `Group=` — UNIX group to run as.
- `remain_after_exit` (Boolean) `RemainAfterExit=` — stay active after main process exits.
- `restart` (String) `Restart=` — `no`, `always`, `on-success`, `on-failure`, `on-abnormal`.
- `restart_sec` (String) `RestartSec=` — delay before restart.
- `standard_error` (String) `StandardError=` — where stderr goes.
- `standard_output` (String) `StandardOutput=` — where stdout goes.
- `timeout_start_sec` (String) `TimeoutStartSec=` — startup timeout.
- `timeout_stop_sec` (String) `TimeoutStopSec=` — stop timeout.
- `type` (String) `Type=` — `simple`, `exec`, `forking`, `oneshot`, `notify`, `idle`.
- `user` (String) `User=` — UNIX user to run as.
- `working_directory` (String) `WorkingDirectory=` — working directory for the process.


<a id="nestedatt--unit"></a>
### Nested Schema for `unit`

Required:

- `description` (String) `Description=` — human-readable description of the unit.

Optional:

- `after` (List of String) `After=` — units to order after.
- `assert` (Attributes) `Assert*=` directives. Failing asserts cause the unit to fail with an error. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--unit--assert))
- `before` (List of String) `Before=` — units to order before.
- `binds_to` (List of String) `BindsTo=` — stronger than Requires; stop if dependency stops.
- `condition` (Attributes) `Condition*=` directives. Failing conditions cause the unit to skip silently. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--unit--condition))
- `conflicts` (List of String) `Conflicts=` — units that cannot run simultaneously.
- `documentation` (List of String) `Documentation=` — URIs referencing documentation.
- `extra` (Map of String) Additional `[Unit]` directives not covered by named attributes. Keys must use exact systemd directive names.
- `part_of` (List of String) `PartOf=` — stop/restart when listed units stop/restart.
- `requires` (List of String) `Requires=` — hard dependencies.
- `wants` (List of String) `Wants=` — soft dependencies.

<a id="nestedatt--unit--assert"></a>
### Nested Schema for `unit.assert`

Optional:

- `directory_not_empty` (List of String) Paths that must be non-empty directories.
- `file_not_empty` (List of String) Paths that must be non-empty files.
- `group` (String) Group (name or GID) that systemd must be running as.
- `host` (String) Hostname or machine ID that must match.
- `path_exists` (List of String) Paths that must exist. Prefix with `!` to negate.
- `path_is_directory` (List of String) Paths that must be directories.
- `path_is_symbolic_link` (List of String) Paths that must be symbolic links.
- `security` (String) Security framework check (e.g. `selinux`, `apparmor`).
- `user` (String) User (name or UID) that systemd must be running as.
- `virtualization` (String) Virtualization type check (e.g. `vm`, `container`, `!container`).


<a id="nestedatt--unit--condition"></a>
### Nested Schema for `unit.condition`

Optional:

- `directory_not_empty` (List of String) Paths that must be non-empty directories.
- `file_not_empty` (List of String) Paths that must be non-empty files.
- `group` (String) Group (name or GID) that systemd must be running as.
- `host` (String) Hostname or machine ID that must match.
- `path_exists` (List of String) Paths that must exist. Prefix with `!` to negate.
- `path_is_directory` (List of String) Paths that must be directories.
- `path_is_symbolic_link` (List of String) Paths that must be symbolic links.
- `security` (String) Security framework check (e.g. `selinux`, `apparmor`).
- `user` (String) User (name or UID) that systemd must be running as.
- `virtualization` (String) Virtualization type check (e.g. `vm`, `container`, `!container`).
